response.justification: The candidate's profile is fundamentally misaligned with the role's primary technical requirement: 8 years of C++ programming experience. C++ does not appear in the candidate's listed skills, and the only documented C++ work is a roughly 6-month stint at National Instruments (2000–2001) and a bachelor's thesis side project. The career is built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. While the candidate shows genuine distributed-systems exposure and a strong testing culture, there is no evidence of cloud compute platform leadership, production cloud architecture ownership at scale, or technical leadership of cloud or DevOps engineers.
strengths: Meaningful exposure to high-throughput distributed systems (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/sec; Optilyz: AWS CDK infrastructure)
Consistent software craftsmanship culture across the career — TDD practice, community workshops, and knowledge-sharing demonstrate engineering maturity
Staff-level trajectory with some infrastructure breadth (Kubernetes, Docker, microservices architecture)
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI circa 2000–2001) against an 8-year minimum — a hard disqualifier on the role's primary language requirement
No documented technical leadership of cloud or DevOps teams; leadership evidence is informal (dojos, workshops, founding a side project)
Core technology stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) is largely orthogonal to the C++-centric, systems-level focus of this role
